items.evidence: Context: Thelwell is describing James Forman's speech at the SNCC Waveland staff retreat (November 1964) and the moment of arriving in the parking lot in UAW-donated Sojourner Motor Fleet cars. The full Whisper string reads 'the text and substanceage in the speech was the polygiotoe, which you had come, he said, sojournal motor fleets.' The crmvet.org publication of this same Thelwell oral history (Civil Rights Movement Veterans, transcript of the 2013 Crosby interview that is the audio source for this Whisper transcription) renders the verbatim sentence as: 'now remember the text and substance of Jim's speech was the poverty from which we had come. Now, he said, "Sojourner Motor Fleet, Sojourner Motor Fleet, Sojourner Motor Fleet," pointing to all these cars and puffing up his chest.' The Whisper rendering 'polygiotoe, which you had come' is a phonetic corruption of 'poverty from which we had come.' The Pass 2 tail-sweep candidate 'the prologue (?)' was the right structural intuition (a noun describing what the speech opened/centered on) but the wrong word; the canonical archival source confirms the noun is 'poverty,' not 'prologue.' Rhetorically this is the speech's contrast: Forman juxtaposed the organization's origins in poverty against the new Sojourner Motor Fleet cars they had driven in.
